to_generate.name: status_to_string
to_generate.doc: Returns a human-readable name for a status code. Useful for logging. The string is library-owned and valid for the lifetime of the process; do not free it. Returns "OCCTL_UNKNOWN" if status is not a recognised value. Thread-safe.

to_generate.name: error_last
to_generate.doc: Returns the thread-local last error. Always returns a non-NULL pointer; an all-zero status field means no error pending on this thread. Valid until the next OCCT-Light call on this thread.

to_generate.name: runtime_occt_version
to_generate.doc: Returns the OCCT version OCCT-Light was built against. Diagnostic only; not part of OCCT-Light's ABI promise. Returns a borrowed pointer to a NUL-terminated UTF-8 string of the form "7.x.y". The string is library-owned and valid for the process lifetime. This function is thread-safe.

to_generate.name: version_major
to_generate.doc: Return the major version number from OCCTL_VERSION_MAJOR.

to_generate.name: version_minor
to_generate.doc: Return the minor version number from OCCTL_VERSION_MINOR.

to_generate.name: version_patch
to_generate.doc: Return the patch version number from OCCTL_VERSION_PATCH.

to_generate.name: occtl_abi_version
to_generate.doc: ABI version. Bumped only on hard breakage. Independent of SemVer. Consumers may compare this against occtl_runtime_abi_version at load time and refuse to proceed on mismatch.

to_generate.name: occtl_error_last
to_generate.doc: Returns a dictionary with the thread-local last error info: status (int), message (String), source_bits (int64_t), extended (int64_t).

to_generate.name: occtl_error_clear
to_generate.doc: Clears the thread-local last error. After calling this, occtl_error_last will return status 0 (OCCTL_OK).

to_generate.name: occtl_error_clear
to_generate.doc:
Clears the thread-local last error.

Calling this is optional — every public function that returns OCCTL_OK already clears the slot. Use it explicitly when you want to assert "no prior failure" before a sequence of calls.

Thread-safe (per-thread).

to_generate.name: occtl_runtime_init_info_version_1
to_generate.doc: Init info versioning constant. New fields are appended under new version constants in future releases.

Configuration struct for occtl_runtime_init(). Construct with OCCTL_RUNTIME_INIT_INFO_INIT or zero-initialise and set struct_version explicitly. Fields: struct_version (must be OCCTL_RUNTIME_INIT_INFO_VERSION_1), p_next (reserved, must be NULL).

to_generate.name: runtime_init_info_init
to_generate.doc: Returns a Dictionary representing the default occtl_runtime_init_info_t values as defined by the OCCTL_RUNTIME_INIT_INFO_INIT macro ({OCCTL_RUNTIME_INIT_INFO_VERSION_1, NULL}).

to_generate.name: runtime_init
to_generate.doc: Initialises the OCCT-Light runtime. Calling this is optional: every entry point lazy-initialises with defaults if needed. Subsequent calls return OCCTL_INVALID_ARGUMENT and have no effect.

to_generate.name: occtl_runtime_abi_version
to_generate.doc: Returns the runtime ABI version. Compare to OCCTL_ABI_VERSION at load time and refuse to load if they differ — that protects against mixing a newer header with an older binary.

to_generate.name: occtl_pi
to_generate.doc: Mathematical constant PI (3.14159265358979323846) shared across public C headers.

to_generate.name: occtl_two_pi
to_generate.doc: 2 * PI constant, approximately 6.28318

to_generate.name: occtl_pi_over_two
to_generate.doc: OCCTL_PI / 2

to_generate.name: occtl_rad_per_deg
to_generate.doc: Radians in one degree.

to_generate.name: occtl_angle_5_deg_rad
to_generate.doc: 5 degrees in radians.

to_generate.name: angle_20_deg_rad
to_generate.doc: 20 degrees in radians.

to_generate.name: occtl_angle_30_deg_rad
to_generate.doc: 30 degrees in radians.

to_generate.name: occtl_angle_1_deg_rad
to_generate.doc: 1 degree in radians. Equals OCCTL_RAD_PER_DEG (OCCTL_PI / 180.0).

to_generate.name: angle_90_deg_rad
to_generate.doc: 90 degrees in radians.

to_generate.name: occtl_ok
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_OK (0): Success.

to_generate.name: occtl_error
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_ERROR (1): Generic failure; see occtl_error_last.

to_generate.name: occtl_invalid_argument
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_INVALID_ARGUMENT (2): A required pointer was NULL or an argument was malformed.

to_generate.name: occtl_invalid_handle
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_INVALID_HANDLE (3): A handle was NULL or had been freed.

to_generate.name: occtl_not_found
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_NOT_FOUND (4): Requested entity does not exist.

to_generate.name: occtl_out_of_memory
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_OUT_OF_MEMORY (5): Allocation failed.

to_generate.name: occtl_out_of_range
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_OUT_OF_RANGE (6): A numeric argument was outside the valid range.

to_generate.name: occtl_not_done
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_NOT_DONE (7): An OCCT operation reported IsDone()==false.

to_generate.name: occtl_geometry_invalid
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_GEOMETRY_INVALID (8): Input geometry was not well-formed.

to_generate.name: occtl_topology_invalid
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_TOPOLOGY_INVALID (9): Input topology was not well-formed.

to_generate.name: occtl_io_error
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_IO_ERROR (10): Filesystem or I/O failure.

to_generate.name: occtl_format_error
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_FORMAT_ERROR (11): File contents were malformed.

to_generate.name: occtl_unsupported
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_UNSUPPORTED (12): Operation not supported in this build/configuration.

to_generate.name: occtl_cancelled
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_CANCELLED (13): A cooperative cancellation was honoured.

to_generate.name: occtl_buffer_too_small
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL (14): Two-call pattern: caller buffer was too small.

to_generate.name: occtl_version_mismatch
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_VERSION_MISMATCH (15): Options struct_version is not supported.

to_generate.name: occtl_internal
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_INTERNAL (16): C++ exception caught at the ABI boundary.

to_generate.name: occtl_wrong_kind
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_WRONG_KIND (17): Handle holds a different kind than the extractor expects.

to_generate.name: occtl_status_reserved_future
to_generate.doc: Returns OCCTL_STATUS_RESERVED_FUTURE (0x7fffffff): Sentinel forcing int32_t storage.

to_generate.name: occtl_succeeded
to_generate.doc: Returns true if the status indicates success (i.e., status equals OCCTL_OK which is 0).

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_invalid
to_generate.doc: Invalid / zero sentinel. Value is 0 so calloc/memset-zero IDs are naturally invalid.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_solid
to_generate.doc: Solid node kind. Value is 1.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_shell
to_generate.doc: Shell node kind. Value is 2.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_face
to_generate.doc: Face node kind. Value is 3.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_wire
to_generate.doc: Wire node kind. Value is 4.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_edge
to_generate.doc: Edge node kind. Value is 5.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_vertex
to_generate.doc: Vertex node kind. Value is 6.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_compound
to_generate.doc: Compound node kind. Value is 7.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_compsolid
to_generate.doc: CompSolid node kind. Value is 8.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_coedge
to_generate.doc: CoEdge (half-edge) node kind. Value is 9.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_product
to_generate.doc: Product (assembly root) node kind. Value is 10.

to_generate.name: occtl_kind_occurrence
to_generate.doc: Occurrence (assembly instance) node kind. Value is 11.

to_generate.name: occtl_node_kind_reserved_future
to_generate.doc: Reserved future sentinel. Value is 0x7fffffff (2147483647). New kinds are appended before this value.

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_invalid
to_generate.doc: Invalid / zero sentinel.

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_surface
to_generate.doc: 3D surface geometry.

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_curve3d
to_generate.doc: 3D curve geometry.

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_curve2d
to_generate.doc: 2D parametric curve on a face (PCurve).

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_triangulation
to_generate.doc: Surface triangulation.

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_polygon3d
to_generate.doc: 3D polyline along an edge.

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_polygon2d
to_generate.doc: 2D polyline.

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_polygon_on_tri
to_generate.doc: Polyline indexed onto a triangulation.

to_generate.name: occtl_rep_kind_reserved_future
to_generate.doc: Reserved future sentinel value.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_invalid
to_generate.doc: Invalid / zero sentinel.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_shell
to_generate.doc: Usage of a Shell definition.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_face
to_generate.doc: Usage of a Face definition.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_wire
to_generate.doc: Usage of a Wire definition.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_coedge
to_generate.doc: Usage of a CoEdge definition.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_vertex
to_generate.doc: Usage of a Vertex definition.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_solid
to_generate.doc: Usage of a Solid definition.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_child
to_generate.doc: Generic mixed-kind child reference.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_occurrence
to_generate.doc: Usage of an Occurrence.

to_generate.name: occtl_ref_kind_reserved_future
to_generate.doc: Reserved future sentinel.

to_generate.name: rep_id_invalid
to_generate.doc: Returns zero-initialized occtl_rep_id_t as the sentinel value for an invalid/empty representation ID.

to_generate.name: rep_id_invalid
to_generate.doc: Invalid sentinel for occtl_rep_id_t, a zero-initialized 64-bit identifier.
Opaque 64-bit representation identity for geometry/mesh data in the graph. The all-zero value is the invalid sentinel.

Persistent unique identity of a graph entity that survives compaction and most node removals, unlike occtl_node_id_t which is session-local. The all-zero value (#OCCTL_UID_INVALID) is the invalid sentinel — compare with bits == 0.

to_generate.name: occtl_uid_invalid
to_generate.doc: Returns the invalid UID sentinel value (all zeros).

to_generate.name: runtime_init_info_init
to_generate.doc: Initialises an occtl_runtime_init_info_t to the default values. Equivalent to the OCCTL_RUNTIME_INIT_INFO_INIT macro but callable from binding languages that cannot use C macros. Returns a Dictionary with status (occtl_status_t as int), struct_version (uint32_t), and p_next (pointer as int64_t).

to_generate.name: occtl_runtime_shutdown
to_generate.doc: Releases process-wide runtime state allocated by #occtl_runtime_init. After this returns, OCCT-Light entry points may still be called — they will lazy-initialise again with defaults. Call this only at process teardown when you want a clean state. @threadsafe No (no other thread may issue OCCT-Light calls during shutdown).

to_generate.name: occtl_uid_wire_size
to_generate.doc: Number of bytes in the wire-format encoding of an #occtl_uid_t. The wire format is a fixed-width opaque blob (16 bytes); do not parse it. The top 8 bytes carry the current 64-bit packed identity in big-endian order; the bottom 8 bytes are reserved (always zero today) so that future widening of the kind tag or counter field can re-key existing stored UIDs without breaking persistence.

to_generate.name: uid_to_bytes
to_generate.doc: Encodes a UID into its 16-byte wire format. Round-trips losslessly through occtl_uid_from_bytes for any valid or invalid UID. Byte order is fixed at big-endian and stable across future ABI versions.

to_generate.name: uid_equal
occtl_uid_from_bytes
to_generate.doc: Decodes a 16-byte wire-format UID back into an #occtl_uid_t. Rejects payloads whose reserved bytes are non-zero (returns #OCCTL_FORMAT_ERROR) — those signal a future wider encoding that this build does not understand. An all-zero input returns #OCCTL_UID_INVALID with #OCCTL_OK. @param[in] in_bytes Borrows it. Must point to a readable buffer of at least #OCCTL_UID_WIRE_SIZE bytes. @param[out] out_uid Borrows it. Must be non-NULL. @retval OCCTL_OK On success. @retval OCCTL_INVALID_ARGUMENT @p in_bytes or @p out_uid is NULL. @retval OCCTL_FORMAT_ERROR Reserved bytes are non-zero. @threadsafe Yes. @sa occtl_uid_to_bytes
